title: ficus pubigera
intro: Ficus pubigera, also known as the hairy fig, is a species of fig tree that belongs to the family Moraceae. Traditionally, the bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ficus pubigera include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and roots are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
